name: Task-Benchmark Definition (任务/基准)
full_intro_template: Paragraph 1 (Motivation): Humans naturally build <cognitive structure> to reason about <environment/task>. Enabling similar capability in AI requires representations beyond <existing representation>.
Paragraph 2 (Task definition): Motivated by this gap, we propose <new task>. Given <input condition>, the objective is to construct <structured output> that captures <property>.

Paragraph 3 (Why hard + why new): Unlike prior settings that evaluate via <downstream proxy>, our task directly evaluates <core object quality>. This requires jointly solving <ability A>, <ability B>, and <ability C> under <constraint>.
Paragraph 4 (Dataset and metric): To facilitate research, we curate <dataset name> with <annotation protocol>, and propose <metric family> to measure <quality dimensions>.

Paragraph 5 (Baseline and findings): We implement a strong baseline based on <pretrained components>, and show that current systems still underperform on <critical dimension>, highlighting room for progress.
Paragraph 6 (Contributions): In summary, our contributions are: (i) <task>; (ii) <dataset + metric>; (iii) <baseline + insight>.
abstract_template: P1: High-level motivation and representation gap.
P2: New task statement and challenge.
sentence_stems: Motivated by this question, we define the task of ...
To facilitate this direction, we curated ... and designed ... metrics.
In summary, our contributions are two-fold/three-fold: ...
